prerequisites: An active HoorayHR account with login credentials (username/email and password) for the workspace whose HR data you want to sync.
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) disabled on the HoorayHR account used for the connection. This is currently a limitation of the HoorayHR API, so the credential login will fail if MFA is enabled.

authMethods.name: Username and password (session token)
authMethods.summary: The connector signs in with your HoorayHR account credentials. It POSTs the username (as email) and password to the HoorayHR authentication endpoint, receives an access token in the response, and sends that token as the Authorization header on all subsequent API requests. You only provide hoorayhrusername and hoorayhrpassword; the connector handles the token exchange automatically. There is no separate API key to generate.

setupSteps.title: Disable MFA on the HoorayHR account
setupSteps.body: Choose the HoorayHR account whose data you want to extract and make sure multi-factor authentication is turned off for it. The HoorayHR API cannot complete the credential login while MFA is enabled, which is a current limitation of the API.

setupSteps.title: Enter your HoorayHR username
setupSteps.body: Set hoorayhrusername to the username (email address) of your HoorayHR account. This value is sent as the email field when the connector authenticates.

setupSteps.title: Enter your HoorayHR password
setupSteps.body: Set hoorayhrpassword to the password for the same HoorayHR account. This field is stored as a secret and is sent together with the username to obtain the access token.

setupSteps.title: Save and verify the connection
setupSteps.body: Save the source. The connector logs in to the HoorayHR API, retrieves an access token, and uses it to read the available streams (users, time-off, leave-types, and sick-leaves). A successful connection check confirms the credentials and token exchange are working.

sources.title: Airbyte source-hoorayhr connector manifest (authenticator and spec)
